title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · Type-System Trace: Production Defense
summary: Tests mock amqplib method calls but never assert routing or failure behavior; deployment starts consumers before topology migration; a reconnect storm follows broker restart; rollback cannot read new message schemas. Planned artifact: Add and verify the production defense increment in tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.
Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 upgrade on supported 4.2 path, Erlang compatibility, feature state, alarms, replica health, client tests, rolling sequence, and rollback limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Production Defense · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5405 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Production Defense contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Production Defense · Theory Production Defense.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Production Defense · Theory Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: Name publisher intent, exchange and routing evidence, queue responsibility, message identity, ownership transfer, byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets, terminal delivery decision, effect, cleanup, and recovery; run a pure deterministic TypeScript model; then change one message or failure and state the amqplib, RabbitMQ, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, or production transfer gate. Apply that move specifically to Production Defense while producing tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Production Defense evidence · Theory Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-ts-production-defense-tlnmox" that uses case 8003 to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqplib or Node built-ins, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24, amqplib 2.0.1, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.
